trade.summary: Spagna-Russia trade collapsed following Russia's 2022 invasion of Ucraina and EU sanctions. Pre-war trade included Russian energy imports, chemicals, and steel; Spanish food products, machinery, and vehicles went the other way. Spagna has effectively ceased Russian energy imports. Remaining trade is minimal non-sanctioned goods.
disputes: Russian hybrid threats to EU territory

diplomatic.summary: Spagna-Russia relations are severely degraded by Russia's Ucraina invasion. Spagna expelled Russian diplomats, imposed sanctions, and is providing military and financial aid to Ucraina. Spagna hosts NATO summits and has been active in coordinating Western response. Russian hybrid operations including disinformation campaigns in Spagna have been documented. Diplomatic channels are minimal.
recent_incidents: Russian disinformation campaigns targeting Spanish public opinion
Alleged Russian interference in Catalan independence movement

military.summary: Spagna and Russia are effectively in an adversarial relationship. Spagna provides military aid to Ucraina and hosts NATO operations. Russia has engaged in hybrid operations against Spagna. Spagna participates in NATO's enhanced forward presence and anti-Russia sanctions enforcement.
incidents: Russian alleged interference in Catalan independence politics

financial.summary: Spanish firms have withdrawn from Russia. Russian assets held by Spanish entities are subject to EU sanctions. Spanish financial system participates in Russian asset freeze mechanisms. Spanish banks have exited Russian operations.

energy.summary: Spagna has ceased Russian energy imports. Spagna was less dependent on Russian gas than Northern/Eastern Europe due to its LNG infrastructure and North African pipeline supply. Spagna successfully transitioned away from any residual Russian energy dependency post-2022. Russian LNG (from Yamal) was historically imported via spot market; this has been reduced.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation suspended. Spanish research institutions have severed most Russian ties. Russian scientists are excluded from EU-funded programs. Joint publications have dropped significantly.
